channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Large amount of dog waste directly on our doorstep., 8th March
item.description:
Someone left a large amount of dog waste directly on our doorstep. This situation has caused significant stress for the residents, especially because we have children and live on the ground floor. it puts us in a particularly vulnerable position - especially the children - due to the potential health risks.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 3, Browning Street, Walworth, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Unlicensed food, 8th March
item.description:
Something needs to be done?? Who is looking after the park ??? Not doing a very good job, even after the boot fair everyone dumps their rubbish in Southwood road what they don’t sell . The person opening and closing the gates should be checking this. You are taking the money but don’t care about us living around here in this mess

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Cole Close, Thamesmead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Sofa dumped on street corner, 8th March
item.description:
As per my previous emails. This corner is problematic. Please collect and let the team dealing with this junction know. Many thanks

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Mountgrove Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Sink abandoned on pavement, 8th March
item.description:
Please collect adap

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Mountgrove Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Mazda with no MOT abandoned outside 43 Wrottesley Road, 8th March
item.description:
Yet another abandoned car on this road. This one has no MOT. Photos taken 8th March 2026

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Wrottesley Road, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Selling food unlicensed??, 8th March
item.description:
Saturday 7 March boot fair day Walking my dog with friends and we have once again people cooking food out of a old oil drum unlicensed and smoking drugs and using the bushes as the toilet ( not nice to see) You have signs all over the park NO UNLICENSED food and you still let them do???

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Cole Close, Thamesmead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dog poo everywhere on the pavements, 8th March
item.description:
Poo everywhere on the pavements
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Church Manorway, Abbey Wood
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish strewn across park, 8th March
item.description:
Rubbish strewn across park, all bins overflowing

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Church Manorway, Abbey Wood
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Graffiti, 8th March
item.description:
Brickwork beneath railway bridge. Appeared overnight. Please prioritise. Many thanks.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 41, Norwood Road, Dulwich Village, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Broken glass, 8th March
item.description:
I smashed vodka bottle is on pathway and has been for a few days

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Springfield Road, Southborough, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pavement sinking, loose slabs, 8th March
item.description:
Pavement slabs outside dental practice are all lifting up and move dramatically when you stand on them. Someone is going to trip up badly on these.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Creek Road, Deptford
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bed flytipped on pavement, 8th March
item.description:
Bed and mattress flytipped blocking pavement on Eagle Wharf Road.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Eagle Wharf Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Hole in pavement, 8th March
item.description:
This hole is large enough for a heel or walking stick to get stuck and cause injury.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Langford Place, Longlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Unresolved chronic littering issue, 8th March
item.description:
People from alleyway of lower Clapton Road continue to fly tip on Powerscroft Road. I would like to be contacted about this matter as it is not being resolved, simply clearing the rubbish is not resolving the problem

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Powerscroft Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Outside of building 4 next to the bike shed, 8th March
item.description:
The road was rebuild, parking spaces were taken and this planter or some sort was made. It impedes access to the pavement it is an eye sore, difficult to cross and serves no purpose. Please fix this by either planting grass and making it a green space or use it for e-scooters/bikes.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 6, St. Augustine's Road, Kentish Town, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Soft left on street, 8th March
item.description:
Abandoned sofa on street

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Hervey Road, Kidbrooke
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large pieces of broken glass scattered along footpath used by school & dog walkers, 8th March
item.description:
Priority please, Sunday 8th March: large thick pieces of broken glass are lying on footpath used by parents taking small/young pupils to entrance for Trinity Primary School. Walking & in buggies. It is dangerous. Enter path from Magpie Hall Lane, glass is scattered from 1st lamp post to 2nd lamp post. Can you please sweep and take away rather than sweeping into verges as injuries could be caused to wildlife & dogs. Many thanks.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Magpie Hall Lane, Bromley Common, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Water gushing onto Council Land, 8th March
item.description:
The waste water pipe from a Private Building is broken and gushing water onto Hackney Council communal land, which is causing a Health and safety issue and an Environmental issue near the Entrance to homes and stairwell.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Lower Clapton Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Graffiti, 8th March
item.description:
Grafitti on the upper part of the M&S building on the High Street Side. It is high up (must have been done by leaning over while on the roof), but it is visible from the road and pavement.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): High Street, Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned vehicle, 8th March
item.description:
This vehicle is taxed but doesn't move. The owner is using it to store tools and comes once or twice a week to load up another car. (Often late at night).

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Minster Road, Plaistow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
